summary: Accept. The OpenSpec artifacts directly address the operator goal by requiring push-first browser delivery via WebSocket, SSE, MCP watch bridging, or equivalent dynamic mechanisms; removing ordinary refresh buttons; inserting new operational updates at the top of relevant views; preserving read-only source-of-truth boundaries; and adding live/reconnecting/stale/fallback/failed status semantics. The task list covers backend live channel work, frontend merge behavior, fallback bridging, refresh-control removal, state preservation, reconnect/idempotency behavior, and no-spend verification. OpenSpec validation passed for dynamic-web-2 with no errors or warnings. Implementation should still pay particular attention to cursor/event-id replay, source-specific fallback visibility, and proving that reconnect/fallback paths do not mutate Hub, Kubernetes, git, or OpenSpec state.
